class.name: PuertsEnvironment
class.brief_description: Creates and manages a Puerts scripting environment backed by a runtime resource.

method.name: initialize
method.description: Initializes the environment with [param backend] and [param string_name_cache_pool]. [param string_name_cache_pool] must be initialized before calling this method. The backend must expose the Puerts function table. If initialization fails, this method returns an [enum Error] value and emits messages through the log callbacks configured by [method set_error_callback], [method set_warn_callback], and [method set_info_callback]. Calling this method on an already initialized environment first disposes the previous runtime.

method.name: dispose
method.description: Releases the current runtime and invalidates all [PuertsScriptValue] objects created by this environment. It is safe to call this method more than once.

method.name: is_alive
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the environment is initialized and has not been disposed.

method.name: get_backend
method.description: Returns the backend object currently attached to this environment, or [code]null[/code] if the environment is not initialized.

method.name: set_error_callback
method.description: Sets the environment error log callback. The callback receives one [String] argument: [code]message[/code].

method.name: set_warn_callback
method.description: Sets the environment warning log callback. The callback receives one [String] argument: [code]message[/code].

method.name: set_info_callback
method.description: Sets the environment info log callback. The callback receives one [String] argument: [code]message[/code].

method.name: eval
method.description: Executes [param code] in the current scripting runtime and returns the result as a [PuertsScriptValue]. Use [param chunk_name] to label the source for stack traces and debugger views. If script evaluation throws an exception, this method returns [code]null[/code] and emits the exception through the error callback. [codeblocks] [gdscript] var value := env.eval("({ name: 'puerts', version: 1 })", "bootstrap.js") var name := value.get_property("name").to_string() print(name) # "puerts" [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

method.name: set_global
method.description: Stores [param value] on the script global object under [param name]. Primitive [Variant] values are converted directly. Godot objects and built-in types are exposed through Puerts' native binding layer.

method.name: get_global
method.description: Returns the script global named [param name] as a [PuertsScriptValue]. If the global does not exist, the returned wrapper usually represents an undefined script value. Call [method PuertsScriptValue.is_undefined] to check for that case.

method.name: tick
method.description: Runs one backend update step. Call this from your frame loop for backends that implement ticking, such as V8 and Node.js.

method.name: low_memory_notification
method.description: Asks the backend to release memory aggressively when the backend supports this operation. This usually maps to a garbage-collection or memory-pressure hook.

method.name: open_debugger
method.description: Opens the backend's remote debugger on the specified [param port]. After opening the debugger, call [method debugger_tick] regularly so the backend can process debugger traffic.

method.name: debugger_tick
method.description: Processes one debugger update step. Call this every frame while a debugger is open. The return value is backend-defined and can be used as a lightweight status result for the tick.

method.name: close_debugger
method.description: Closes the active remote debugger, if the backend supports debugging.

method.name: terminate_execution
method.description: Requests that the backend stop the currently running script. Only some backends implement forced termination. Unsupported backends emit an error through the configured error callback.
